Cornell United Religious Work records, 1869-19721965-1972
Collection Number: 39-2-1468

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Cornell United Religious Work records, 1869-1972 1965-1972
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
39-2-1468
Abstract:
Records of the Cornell University Christian Association.
Creator:
Cornell United Religious Work
Quanitities:
89.5 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
NOTES

Includes collection #39-2-668, #39-2-1267, 39-2-1268 and 39-2-1604.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Cornell United Religious Work records, #39-2-1468. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content

Records of the Cornell University Christian Association include minutes of the Board of Directors, business meetings, the Executive Committee, and other committees; constitution and bylaws; officers and members; financial records; scrapbooks; and office files of Richard Henry Edwards, Director. Records of Cornell United Religious Work include minutes of the Board of Control; office files of Richard H. Edwards, Director, 1930-1939, James A. G. Moore, Acting Director, 1937-1939, and William W. Mendenhall, 1939-1943; scrapbooks; and correspondence relating to the functions and activities of CURW, 1932-1933. Also, tape recordings of the National Association of College Chaplains Conference, 1965; portraits; memorabilia; and miscellaneous reports, books, clippings, pamphlets, photograph albums, and periodicals relating to CURW activities.
Scope and content

Includes records of the East Harlem Project (1965-68), POWER Project files (1968), and the Cornell in Central America Project (1953-1969); and a report on the Brazil '65 Project, designed to interest North American students in the development issues of Latin America.
Access Restrictions:

Access to collections #39-2-1267, "Power" Project 1968 files and #39-2-1268, East Harlem Project, 1965-1968 files restricted to the permission of the office of origin.
Related Materials

See also Cornell United Religious Work. Sage Chapel Records, #39-2-1300 for programs, sermons, and other relateid items.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Edwards, Richard Henry, 1877-1954.
Moore, James A. G.
Mendenhall, William W.
Cornell University -- : Students.
Cornell University Christian Association
National Association of College Chaplains
Places:
Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
Subjects:
Universities and colleges -- Religion.
